name: Elbow MRI
description: MRI of the elbow for evaluation of ligaments, tendons, and joint pathology

findings.technique: MRI of the elbow was performed in multiple planes including T1, T2, PD, and STIR sequences.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Lateral Collateral Ligament
sections.content: The LCL (lateral ulnar collateral ligament) demonstrates [intact / no tear / no sprain]. The ligament is [taut / normal]

sections.name: Medial Collateral Ligament
sections.content: The MCL demonstrates [intact / no tear / no sprain]. The anterior bundle is [intact]

sections.name: Common Extensor Origin
sections.content: The common extensor origin demonstrates [no tear / no tendinopathy / no lateral epicondylitis]. The extensor tendons are [intact]

sections.name: Common Flexor Origin
sections.content: The common flexor origin demonstrates [no tear / no tendinopathy / no medial epicondylitis]. The flexor tendons are [intact]

sections.name: Biceps Tendon
sections.content: The biceps tendon demonstrates [no tear / no tendinopathy]. The distal biceps tendon is [intact]

sections.name: Triceps Tendon
sections.content: The triceps tendon demonstrates [no tear / no tendinopathy]. The tendon is [intact]

sections.name: Joint
sections.content: The elbow joint demonstrates [no effusion / no osteochondral lesion / no cartilage defect]. No joint effusion.

sections.name: Bone
sections.content: The humerus, radius, and ulna demonstrate [no fracture / no bone bruise / no osteochondral lesion].

impression:
1. Normal elbow MRI.
2. No ligamentous injury, tendon tear, or osteochondral lesion identified.
author: RadReport Templates
